title: woodsia glabella subsp. pulchella
intro: Woodsia glabella subsp. pulchella, also known as the beaked woodsia or pulchella beaked woodsia, is a rare, perennial fern species native to the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antimicrobial agent, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. The herbal preparations of Woodsia glabella subsp. pulchella typically involve drying and processing the plant's leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or powders for medicinal use.
